snippets.title: Iterative binary search (half-open)
snippets.explanation: Half-open intervals make boundary reasoning easier and remove the classic off-by-one mistakes.

snippets.title: Use bisect, don't roll your own
snippets.code:
import bisect

scores = [0.10, 0.32, 0.55, 0.71, 0.80, 0.93]

idx = bisect.bisect_left(scores, 0.55)        # 2 — index of 0.55
ins = bisect.bisect_left(scores, 0.60)        # 3 — where 0.60 would go

bisect.insort(scores, 0.60)                    # insert preserving order
print(scores)   # [0.10, 0.32, 0.55, 0.60, 0.71, 0.80, 0.93]
snippets.explanation: bisect is in CPython, written in C, and handles all the boundary cases. Use it.

snippets.title: Range query in O(log n)
snippets.code:
import bisect

times = sorted([1.1, 2.0, 2.5, 3.7, 4.2, 5.8])

def in_range(times, t0, t1):
    lo = bisect.bisect_left(times, t0)
    hi = bisect.bisect_right(times, t1)
    return times[lo:hi]

print(in_range(times, 2.0, 4.0))   # [2.0, 2.5, 3.7]
snippets.explanation: Two O(log n) probes + one slice — beats any linear filter once the list is large.

snippets.title: Binary-search the answer (monotonic predicate)
snippets.explanation: Whenever an answer space has a yes/no test that is monotonic in k, binary search beats sweeping.
